framework_bugs.summary: init.sh --non-interactive sets poc_mode='production' for --gov-mode production, which blocks start-phase4
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash init.sh --non-interactive --project x --platform web --track light --deployment organizational --gov-mode production --language typescript --project-dir /tmp/x --git-host other --remote-url https://example.com/fake.git --branch-protection-attested --visibility private --allow-existing-dir; then jq .poc_mode .claude/phase-state.json; bash scripts/process-checklist.sh --start-phase4
framework_bugs.expected: For --gov-mode production, poc_mode should be null (production scenario should not be a POC). The interactive flow at init.sh:381 sets POC_MODE="" for Production. The non-interactive flow at init.sh:3217 sets POC_MODE="$GOV_MODE" unconditionally so production becomes 'production', and start_phase4 (process-checklist.sh:540) blocks any non-null poc_mode. start-phase4 should succeed.
framework_bugs.actual: phase-state.json contains "poc_mode": "production"; start_phase4 prints '[FAIL] Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in production mode' and exits 1. Workaround: run scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production to clear poc_mode. This is a regression introduced by PR #19 (BL-016) — the interactive parity branch correctly maps Production->empty, but the non-interactive path does not.

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: U-D confirmation: no script auto-advances .current_phase after gate passes; current_phase remained 0 throughout the run

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script advances current_phase after gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: Complete all phase1_architecture steps, run check-phase-gate.sh, then jq .current_phase .claude/phase-state.json
framework_bugs.expected: current_phase should advance from 0 to 1 (or analogous) after Phase 1 gate passes; documented procedure must include an advance command or check-phase-gate must mutate state.
framework_bugs.actual: current_phase remained 0 after all Phase 1 steps complete and gate reported consistent. Same observed at every gate transition through the entire run.

documentation_gaps.gap: No documented procedure exists for advancing .current_phase after a gate passes; check-phase-gate.sh only reports consistency and never mutates phase-state.json. Users rely on intake-wizard.sh / upgrade-project.sh side-effects.

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): stub feature for UAT' returned exit 0 after all 5 build-loop steps complete

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: U-C confirmation: direct shell `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop'` returned exit 0 (bypassed) because process-checklist.sh check_commit_message exits 0 when current_phase < 2 (lines 1049-1052). Since U-D blocks current_phase advancement to 2+, BL-006 is effectively unreachable through this path. PreToolUse hook still works but only when invoked through Claude Code, not direct shell.

tests.name: bl006_grace_window_resets_after_record
tests.details: After --record-feature uat-feat-1, started uat-feat-2, completed all 5 steps, and `git commit -m 'feat(uat-2): second feature'` succeeded. PR #17 grace-window fix appears intact.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: Created sentinel via scripts/pending-approval.sh --offer; piped synthetic PreToolUse payload {"tool_input":{"command":"git commit -m '...'"}} to scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh; got hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision='deny' with reason citing pending question. Direct shell `git commit` bypasses (expected — gate is PreToolUse-only).

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed sentinel; subsequent PreToolUse payload returned no deny.

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: .features_completed = ['uat-feat-1','uat-feat-2'] in .claude/build-progress.json

framework_bugs.summary: U-C confirmation: direct shell `git commit` with feat: subject bypasses BL-006
framework_bugs.reproduction: From a fresh project, do not start a build_loop; run `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(x): no loop'`; commit succeeds.
framework_bugs.expected: BL-006 should block feat: commits when no Build Loop is in progress, regardless of invocation path.
framework_bugs.actual: Commit succeeds. Two contributing reasons: (1) check_commit_message early-returns when current_phase<2 (and current_phase never advances per U-D), (2) PreToolUse hooks fire only inside Claude Code, not in a direct shell. Confirmed open from prior sweep.

summary: Section A/B/C/D/E ran end-to-end on production/web/light. PR #17/#18/#19 fixes verified (helpers copied, framework-self guard active, BL-015 sentinel blocks/resolves, BL-006 grace window resets, no framework contamination). Found 1 High regression introduced by PR #19: --non-interactive --gov-mode production sets poc_mode='production' (instead of null), which blocks start_phase4 until upgrade-project.sh --to-production is run. U-C and U-D confirmed still open.
